channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-781] Kernel update [RHEL6.2 2.6.32-220.el6]
item.description:
We have early access to the RHEL6.2 kernel, and we have identified a few things that needed fixing to allow lustre and ldiskfs to compile. The following two lustre bugs have patches against master:

LU-766 - Incorrect HAVE_STATFS_DENTRY_PARAM result
LU-778 - RHEL 6.2: rdma_create_id() takes four arguments

ldiskfs needed several patches refreshed (at minimum, I did not look very closely to see if ext4 functionality changed). I made a rhel6.2 series and new patch files for any that needed refreshing. They can be found in our external ldiskfs package:

https://github.com/chaos/ldiskfs

in commit d7af3f4.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Landed for 2.1.1 and 2.2

Hi, Peter,

I'll file a new ticket for 2.6.32-220.4.1 kernel once i can access Jira.
It seem has some access problem from my network.

Regards,
YangSheng

YangSheng

Wouldn't it be better to open a new ticket for the latest kernel upgrade?

Peter
Move forward to next update version. [2.6.32-220.4.1.el6]

Integrated in lustre-master » i686,client,el5,ofa #442
LU-781 kernel: kernel update [RHEL6.2 2.6.32-220] (Revision d4a913c01f86ed080aebd5ce0c262483ad187949)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : d4a913c01f86ed080aebd5ce0c262483ad187949
Files :

  • lustre/kernel_patches/which_patch

Result = SUCCESS

I just logged on to rhel6-32-build01 and was able to run:

$ yumdownloader --destdir . kernel-devel-2.6.32-220.el6Loaded plugins: fastestmirror
Determining fastest mirrors
 * base: linux.mirrors.es.net
 * extras: centos.mirrors.hoobly.com
 * updates: centos.mirror.facebook.net
kernel-devel-2.6.32-220.el6.i686.rpm                     | 7.2 MB     00:22

as myself with no problem. So I wonder what's stopping jenkins from doing the same thing (by way of lbuild of course).

Unfortunately the output of the yumdownloader command is being discarded by lbuild, along with lbuild's assumption that yumdownloader will always succeed given the lack of error checking. Clearly that command needs some error checking (i.e. in find_linux_rpm-rhel()) including possibly dumping the output of the yumdownloader command in the case of failure.

The failure was isolated to the rhel6-32-1 build node – there was a problem with an outdated yum config entry pointing at newbuild.whamcloud.com causing yumdownloader to fail. I removed that entry and retriggered the build. Should proceed now.

Mike

Hi, Mike, Looks like the build still failed.

http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/4105/
Many thanks, Mike, I'll resubmit build request.

Hmm. Not sure what the issue is, offhand. I logged into the r6-32-1 builder and ran that yumdownloader command manually and it worked fine. The problem isn't with downloading a kernel SRPM, it's with downloading a kernel-devel binary RPM for patchless client builds. Will look into this further, but it might be a lbuild issue.

Hi, Brian, Mike, could you please take a look for the issue i have post above? Seem the srpms cannot download from Redhat's site.

This is last link: http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/4100/
Sounds like a problem with your build farm. You should probably open a separate bug on that.

I am not sure that below message indicated some issue. But it really block me.

+++ local sudo=
++++ lsb_release -s -i
+++ '[' CentOS = RedHatEnterpriseServer ']'
+++ yumdownloader --destdir /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/yumC1onEt kernel-devel-2.6.32-220.el6
++++ cd /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/yumC1onEt
++++ echo '*'
+++ local 'rpm=*'
+++ mv '/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/yumC1onEt/*' /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686
mv: cannot stat `/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/yumC1onEt/*': No such file or directory
+++ rmdir /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/yumC1onEt
+++ echo '*'
+++ return 0
++ found_rpm='*'
++ found_rpm='/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
++ ret=0
++ echo '/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
++ return 0
+ kernelrpm='/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
+ lnxrel=220.el6
+ unpack_linux_devel_rpm '/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*' -
+ local 'kernelrpm=/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
+ '[' -f '/var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*' ']'
+ return 255
+ fatal 1 'Could not find the Linux tree in /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
+ cleanup
+ true
+ error 'Could not find the Linux tree in /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
+ local 'msg=Could not find the Linux tree in /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'
+ '[' -n 'Could not find the Linux tree in /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*' ']'
+ echo -e '\nlbuild: Could not find the Linux tree in /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*'

lbuild: Could not find the Linux tree in /var/lib/jenkins/lbuild-data/kernelrpm/2.6.32/rhel6/i686/*

http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/3947/arch=i686,build_type=client,distro=el6,ib_stack=inkernel/console

YangSheng

I think that you are the best candidate for this work

Thanks

Peter
